river in Virginia, United States

description: private university in Washington, D.C., USA
description_wiki: The George Washington University (GW or GWU) is a private federally chartered research university in Washington, D.C., United States.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: American ammunition ship
description_wiki: The first USS Nitro (AE–2) was an ammunition ship of the United States Navy, commissioned from 1921 to 1945.

description: large canal in Panama
description_wiki: The Panama Canal (Spanish: Canal de Panamá) is an artificial 82-kilometer (51-mile) waterway in Panama that connects the Caribbean Sea with the Pacific Ocean.

description_wiki: A boatswain's mate is a petty officer assisting the boatswain aboard ship.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: historic park in San Diego, California
description_wiki: Balboa Park is a 1,200-acre (490 ha) historic urban cultural park in San Diego, California.

description: severe economic crisis in the USA in the 1930s
description_wiki: In the United States, the Great Depression is commonly (though not universally) dated to the Wall Street crash of October 1929.

description: Founding Father, U.S. president from 1797 to 1801
description_wiki: John Adams (October 30, 1735 – July 4, 1826) was a Founding Father and the second president of the United States from 1797 to 1801.

description: United States military decoration
description_wiki: The Silver Star Medal (SSM) is the United States Armed Forces' third-highest military decoration for valor in combat.

description: 1918 Altair-class destroyer tender
description_wiki: USS Rigel (AD-13/ARb-1/AR-11) was an Altair class destroyer tender named for Rigel, the brightest star in the constellation Orion.

description: 1912 New York-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Texas (BB-35) is a museum ship in Galveston, Texas, and former United States Navy New York-class battleship.

description: bay in Puntarenas Province, Costa Rica
description_wiki: Coronado Bay is a body of water located on the southwestern Pacific coast of Costa Rica.
